name: IFSCA GIFT City Tokenization Framework

issuer.name: International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA)
issuer.type: Indian unified financial regulator for IFSC

regulator.name: IFSCA (unified regulator for banking, capital markets, insurance, fund management in IFSC)

summary: India's GIFT City (Gujarat International Finance Tec-City) operates as an offshore financial center with regulatory autonomy under IFSCA. Multiple tokenization initiatives announced: tokenized fund administration framework (2024), regulatory sandbox accepting tokenization use cases, and proposals for tokenized fund issuance for foreign investors targeting Indian assets. Significant as India's regulated entry point into tokenization — bypassing the unresolved regulatory ambiguity of mainland Indian crypto policy. Provides infrastructure for tokenized funds targeting NRI / GCC investor base.
underlying_asset: Various — sandbox covers tokenized fund units, debt securities, real estate-backed instruments.
reserve_composition: Per participant; framework requires backing disclosure but does not standardize.
secondary_market: Within IFSC ecosystem; cross-border subject to RBI/SEBI coordination.

timeline.event: Regulatory sandbox formally accepting tokenization applications
First tokenized fund pilots reportedly under IFSCA review (specifics not publicly disclosed)
